description_long: This is a robust, wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertically aligned wooden staves, held together by two prominent, rust-colored metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The top surface is flat and appears to be sealed with a wooden lid, while the sides feature large, round metal rivets and a single metal ring attached to the upper hoop, likely for handling or securing the barrel. The wood grain is visible, giving the object a rustic, weathered appearance, and the metal bands show signs of oxidation, suggesting outdoor or heavy-duty use. There are no visible labels, engravings, or decorative markings, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A sturdy, wooden barrel with metal hoops, a flat wooden lid, and a single metal ring for handling. The surface is rustic and weathered, with visible wood grain and oxidized metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metal ring attached to the upper hoop and the detailed wood grain on the staves.
description_view_1: Side view with the metal ring less visible, highlighting the round metal rivets and the curvature of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with no visible ring, focusing on the continuous wood and metal banding.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the metal ring on the right side, with clear visibility of the metal hoops and rivets.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
